_purpose: Concept cards rendered above the quest chains on the onboarding page. Order in this file is the display order. Edit text without recompiling.

concepts.title: Your DID is your identity
concepts.body: A post-quantum cryptographic key on your device. No username, no password. Backup is a 24-word phrase in Settings. Lose your phone? Recover through guardians you pick — friends and family who hold encrypted shares.

concepts.title: Credentials prove what's true about you
concepts.body: Schools, employers, communities, even individual humans can issue Verifiable Credentials. You hold them, you choose when to share. No central registry, no single point of revocation.

concepts.title: Trust grows from what you do
concepts.body: A multi-layer trust score combines your vouches, credentials, activity, and age. Anti-Sybil math makes farms count for less. Inputs are always visible — no black-box reputation.

concepts.title: Anyone can host a server
concepts.body: The network is federated. No central owner. If one server goes down, the rest keep working. Each server runs local governance; civilization-scope changes need federation-wide quorum.
